Chromium exposure can come from industrial work, dietary supplements, or environmental contamination. This urine test for chromium levels helps measure the amount of chromium in urine to detect possible exposure. It measures how much chromium your body is excreting, reflecting both exposure and excretion efficiency. 

Why Chromium Test Through Urine Important?

Urine chromium testing is useful for monitoring chronic exposure and evaluating the body’s ability to clear excess chromium. In addition to urine testing, the Plasma Chromium testing offers further insight into chromium levels in the body.

What Gets Assessed?

  • Chromium levels in urine

A Homocysteine blood test is a vital tool for understanding your cardiovascular and cognitive health. High levels of homocysteine, an amino acid, are linked to several serious health issues, including fatigue, poor concentration, mood changes, and a higher risk of cardiovascular disease. This simple blood test measures homocysteine concentration, providing valuable insight into your overall health.

Why Blood Test for Homocysteine Levels Matters?

Homocysteine is an amino acid that should be kept within a healthy range. Elevated levels may indicate deficiencies in B6, B12, or folate, and are strongly associated with cardiovascular risk, cognitive decline, and impaired detoxification. Routine homocysteine lab testing provides crucial insight into your nutritional status and helps prevent disease through early intervention.

What Gets Assessed?

  • Homocysteine concentration in blood (cardiovascular and methylation health marker)

Turnaround Time

  • 24 Hours

If you experience fatigue, sensitivity to chemicals, hormonal imbalances, or slow digestion, your liver’s detox pathways may be under strain. The Hepatic Detoxification Profile evaluates how efficiently your liver functions and clears toxins, providing insights into both Phase I and Phase II detoxification pathways.

It measures the levels of D-glucaric and mercapturic acids in the urine to identify exposure to xenobiotics and assess liver function. This urine test helps identify imbalances that can contribute to toxin build-up, poor energy, or long-term health risks.

Why Hepatic Detoxification Profile (HDP) Matters?

Your liver is the body’s main detox organ, breaking down and eliminating harmful substances. Imbalances in detox pathways can affect hormone regulation, energy production, and resilience against environmental toxins. Understanding your liver detox capacity with HDP testing helps tailor nutrition and lifestyle strategies to support optimal health and long-term protection.

What Gets Assessed?

  • D-Glucaric Acid (Phase I)
  • Mercapturic Acids (Phase II)
  • Creatinine

Turnaround Time

  • 18 Days

Epigenetic changes in DNA methylation are key indicators of biological ageing and long-term health. The DNA Methylation Profile - OSM analyses key genes like BHMT, MTHFR, COMT, CHDH, DHFR, FOLH1, GNMT, and MTR to uncover hidden imbalances that can contribute to premature ageing and increased risk of health issues. 

Why DNA Methylation Profile Testing Matters?

The DNA Methylation Test UK is a comprehensive epigenetic tool that looks beyond nutrition, measuring global DNA methylation patterns and ageing markers. With the DNA methylation analysis, you can compare your biological age with chronological age and develop personalised strategies for prevention and healthy longevity.

For a more nutrition-focused analysis of methylation pathways, consider taking DNA Methylation Profile -NUT10, which provides insights into nutrient-related genetic influences.

What Gets Assessed?

  • Specific genes like BHMT, MTHFR, COMT, CHDH, DHFR, FOLH1, GNMT, and MTR 
  • Epigenetic aging markers (biological vs chronological age)
  • Methylation imbalance (hypo- vs hypermethylation)
  • Disease susceptibility and longevity indicators

Methylation plays a vital role in detoxification, energy production, and maintaining cardiovascular health. The DNA Methylation Profile - NUT10 is a swab sample DNA test that provides a far more complex and detailed assessment compared to a standard blood test.

Unlike instant blood-based methylation screens, this advanced swab test requires a laboratory culture to be developed, which means the turnaround time is longer, but the insights are deeper.

The NUT10 profile evaluates genetic and biochemical factors that influence methylation, including folate and vitamin B12 pathways. Imbalances may lead to fatigue, high homocysteine, poor detoxification capacity, and chronic health risks. 

Why DNA Methylation Profile - NUT10 Matters?

DNA methylation analysis via swab testing offers a detailed look at pathways linked to nutrition, energy metabolism, and detoxification. It highlights genetic and functional imbalances that may increase cardiovascular, neurological, or toxin-related risks. With the results, individuals can optimise their methyl donor intake (folate, B12, B6, betaine) and adopt targeted lifestyle strategies for long-term health.

For a basic evaluation of methylation markers, you can book the  DNA Methylation Profile - OSM.

What Gets Assessed?

  • Folate and vitamin B12-dependent methylation pathways
  • Homocysteine metabolism
  • Nutritional and genetic influences on methylation
  • Detoxification-related methylation efficiency

Turnaround Time

  • 11 Days

Imbalances in neurotransmitters can cause mood swings, anxiety, insomnia, poor focus, or stress-related disorders. This Neurotransmitter urine profile measures a broad range of transmitters to assess chemical balance in the brain and nervous system, providing valuable insight into neurological function.

Why Urine Profile for Neurotransmitters Matters?

Neurotransmitters regulate mood, energy, cognitive health, and sleep. Imbalances caused by stress, diet, or health conditions can impact mental and physical health. Our neurotransmitter imbalance test helps identify these imbalances and offers insights into symptoms like fatigue, depression, and sleep disorders.

What Gets Assessed in Neuro Advanced Profile?

The Comprehensive Neurotransmitter Urine Test measures the urinary levels of major neurotransmitters and their key intermediates, including:

  • Excitatory Neurotransmitters: Dopamine, Noradrenaline, Adrenaline, Glutamate
  • Inhibitory Neurotransmitters: Serotonin, GABA, Glycine
  • Other Key Markers: Histamine, PEA (Phenylethylamine), and Taurine

The Coenzyme Q10 test measures blood levels of this vital antioxidant, crucial for energy production within cells and protecting against oxidative stress. Low CoQ10 levels may be linked to chronic fatigue, muscle weakness, neurological issues, or heart disease. This blood test for Coenzyme Q10 level is often recommended for individuals taking statins, who have mitochondrial disorders, or those experiencing extreme tiredness or cardiovascular concerns.

Why Need a Coenzyme Q10 Blood Test?

CoQ10 is essential for mitochondrial energy production and cellular protection. Deficiency may contribute to cardiovascular disease, neurodegenerative conditions, and reduced physical performance. CoQ10 Testing helps identify whether supplementation or further medical investigation is needed, supporting energy balance, heart health, and long-term wellness.

What Gets Assessed?

  • Blood concentration of Coenzyme Q10 (ubiquinone/ubiquinol levels)

The Ceruloplasmin test measures levels of this copper-carrying protein, which plays a vital role in iron metabolism and antioxidant defence. Abnormal levels may indicate copper deficiency, Wilson’s disease, Menkes disease, or liver disorders. Symptoms such as unexplained fatigue, neurological issues, anaemia, or jaundice may prompt this test to assess copper balance and overall metabolic health.

Why Ceruloplasmin Blood Test Matters?

Ceruloplasmin is essential for transporting copper and regulating iron in the body. Low levels can point to serious conditions like Wilson’s disease or severe copper deficiency, while high levels may indicate inflammation or liver dysfunction. A ceruloplasmin lab test helps identify underlying metabolic, hepatic, or genetic disorders, allowing for timely intervention and treatment planning.

What Gets Assessed?

  • Ceruloplasmin concentration (a copper-binding protein)

Turnaround Time

  • 24 Hours

The Casomorphin and Gliadorphin Peptides test detects abnormal peptide fragments derived from incomplete digestion of casein (milk protein) and gluten (wheat protein). Elevated levels may indicate impaired gut function or “leaky gut” and have been linked to behavioural, neurological, and digestive symptoms such as brain fog, hyperactivity, bloating, or food sensitivities. This test helps identify whether gluten or dairy peptides may be impacting health.

Why Casomorphin and Gliadorphin Peptides Test Matters?

Incomplete digestion of gluten and casein can release bioactive peptides that act like opioids in the body, potentially influencing mood, behaviour, and gut-brain interactions. Measuring these peptides provides insight into hidden food-related triggers, especially in individuals with autism spectrum conditions, ADHD, chronic digestive complaints, or unexplained fatigue. Early detection allows for targeted dietary and therapeutic strategies.

What Gets Assessed?

This test typically measures:

  • Beta-Casomorphin-7 (from casein/milk)
  • Gliadorphin (from gluten/wheat)
  • Related peptide levels indicating incomplete protein breakdown

Turnaround Time

  • 10 Days

The Complete Cardiometabolic Risk Profile assesses key biomarkers linked to heart health, blood sugar regulation, and metabolic balance by evaluating cholesterol, glucose control, and inflammatory markers. Cardiometabolic screening via blood test is particularly valuable for those with family history, lifestyle risks, or symptoms such as fatigue, weight changes, or high blood pressure.

Why Cardiometabolic Risk Profile Matters?

Modern lifestyles, poor diet, and stress contribute significantly to rising rates of diabetes and cardiovascular disease. The Cardiometabolic Profile provides an in-depth view of heart and metabolic function, highlighting imbalances before they progress into serious health problems.

For men seeking a broader picture of their health, this test can be combined with a Well Man Test for a detailed assessment.

What Gets Assessed?

  • Lipid panel (total cholesterol, HDL, LDL, triglycerides)
  • Blood glucose and HbA1c (long-term glucose control)
  • Insulin resistance markers
  • High-sensitivity C-reactive protein (hs-CRP) for inflammation
  • Homocysteine levels
  • Additional metabolic indicators as per the laboratory panel

The Antioxidant Profile measures your body’s ability to counteract oxidative stress, which can damage cells, accelerate ageing, and contribute to chronic disease. Comprehensive Antioxidant Profile evaluates the balance between antioxidants and free radicals, providing insights into nutritional adequacy, immune resilience, and risk of degenerative conditions.

Why Antioxidant Blood Test Profile Matters?

Oxidative stress plays a key role in ageing, inflammation, and the progression of many chronic illnesses, including cardiovascular disease, diabetes, and neurodegenerative disorders. The Comprehensive Antioxidant Profile highlights deficiencies in protective compounds and imbalances that weaken the body’s natural defences. By identifying gaps in antioxidant status, this test helps optimise nutrition, improve cellular protection, and support overall wellbeing.

What Gets Assessed?

This test typically measures:

  • Enzymatic antioxidants such as superoxide dismutase, glutathione peroxidase, and catalase
  • Non-enzymatic antioxidants including vitamins A, C, and E
  • Glutathione levels, the body’s master antioxidant
  • Coenzyme Q10 and other key nutrients supporting cellular defence
  • Markers of oxidative damage, such as lipid peroxides or DNA oxidation indicators

Turnaround Time

  • 6 Days

Amino acids are the building blocks of protein and play essential roles in energy production, neurotransmitter balance, detoxification, and tissue repair. This Amino Acids Analysis Urine Test helps detect rare disorders related to amino acid transport, such as cystinuria, lysinuric protein intolerance, and Hartnup disease. For kidney stone investigations or suspected cystinuria, it’s best to request a Urine Cystine Test.

Why Need a Urine Amino Acids Profile?

The Amino Acids (Urine) Test helps identify metabolic and transport disorders that affect how your body processes amino acids. Detecting these issues early can prevent complications like kidney stones, poor growth, or metabolic imbalances. It also supports accurate diagnosis of conditions like cystinuria or Hartnup disease. 

Collecting a plasma amino acid sample alongside is recommended to ensure accurate diagnosis.

What Gets Assessed?

This test evaluates a broad spectrum of amino acids and related metabolites in urine, including:

  • Essential and non-essential amino acids such as leucine, isoleucine, lysine, methionine, valine, and tryptophan
  • Neurotransmitter precursors like glutamine, glycine, and tyrosine
  • Markers of protein digestion and metabolism, such as alanine, ornithine, and citrulline
  • Indicators of oxidative stress, detoxification, and inflammation, including homocystine, cysteine, and taurine
  • Key nutrient-linked amino acids reflecting B6, B12, folate, magnesium, and zinc status

Turnaround Time

  • 14 Days

Allergic reactions to antibiotics can cause rashes, hives, swelling, or, in severe cases, life-threatening anaphylaxis. This β-Lactamines Antibiotics Allergy Panel identifies IgE-mediated sensitivities to commonly prescribed β-lactam antibiotics, including penicillins and cephalosporins. It is particularly important for patients with suspected drug allergies to ensure safe prescribing and to avoid unnecessary avoidance of essential treatments.

Why β-Lactamines Antibiotics Allergy Assessment Matters?

Antibiotic allergies are often over-reported, leading to restricted treatment options and reliance on second-line drugs. By identifying true IgE-mediated allergies, this test helps distinguish between intolerance and genuine hypersensitivity. Early detection ensures safer prescribing, optimises treatment choices, and reduces the risk of severe allergic reactions.

What Gets Assessed?

  • Penicillin G
  • Penicillin V
  • Ampicillin
  • Amoxicillin
  • Cephalosporin mix (Cefaclor, Cefaxin, Cefotaxim, Cefuroxim)

Symptoms such as hives, swelling, digestive upset, or breathing difficulties after eating eggs may be signs of an egg allergy. Although the main allergens in egg are found in the egg white, egg yolk also contains a large number of specific IgE-binding allergens.

The Egg Allergy Panel (IgE) helps identify allergic reactions to different components of eggs, distinguishing between sensitivities to egg white, yolk, or specific proteins, enabling more accurate management of dietary restrictions. 

Why is an Egg Allergy Test (IgE) needed?

Egg allergy is one of the most common food allergies in children, though it can also affect adults. Since egg proteins are widely used in foods and even vaccines, identifying the specific protein triggering the allergy is essential for avoiding accidental exposure and preventing mild to severe allergic reactions.

What Gets Assessed?

Mild to Severe Egg allergies and sensitivities for:

  • Egg’s white
  • Egg’s yolk
  • Ovomucoid
  • Ovalbumin
  • Conalbumin
  • Lysozyme

Symptoms such as hives, stomach upset, breathing difficulties, or swelling after eating seafood may indicate an allergy. The Seafood Allergy Panel (IgE) screens for common shellfish and fish allergens, helping you identify specific triggers and reduce the risk of unexpected allergic reactions.

What Gets Assessed?

Our private Lactose Intolerance Test offers a quick, easy, and accurate way to assess whether you are lactose intolerant.

column2: Advanced Thyroid Test: At-home Thyroid Function Blood Test
column3:
The Thyroid gland plays a crucial role in regulating your metabolism, energy, and overall physical and cognitive health. Our Advanced Thyroid Test measures the levels of thyroid hormones (TSH, FT4, FT3) and detects antibodies; the key biomarkers of autoimmune thyroid disorder.

If you are experiencing unexplained weight changes, fatigue, depression, or hair problems, you must get your thyroid level tested. The private thyroid test by The National Blood Test Clinic UK evaluates T3, T4, and TSH levels to assess how well thyroid function can be regulated.

Why Get Tested?

Thyroid disorders are common, especially in women, but they can develop in men too, and are often hard to diagnose due to overlapping symptoms with other conditions. 

An underactive thyroid (Hypothyroidism) may lead to tiredness, weight gain, hair loss, depression, and cold sensitivity. On the other hand, an overactive thyroid (Hyperthyroidism) can result in unexplained weight loss, anxiety, mood swings, trouble sleeping, heat sensitivity, muscle weakness, itchiness, and frequent urination.

If you're experiencing any of these symptoms, our private thyroid test can help you gain clarity. This thyroid test kit is designed to perform convenient at-home testing to check your thyroid levels, including T3, T4, and TSH.

What Gets Tested

This test checks three thyroid hormones and two key thyroid-related antibodies to provide a comprehensive overview of thyroid function and potential autoimmune issues.

Markers included:

  • TFTP - Thyroid Panel;
    • Free Thyroxine (FT4)
    • Free Triiodothyronine (FT3)
    • Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH)
  • TGAB - Thyroglobulin Antibodies
  • TPO - Thyroid Peroxidase Antibodies

column2: Cholesterol Test | At-Home Cholesterol Blood Test
column3:
The blood test for cholesterol is a comprehensive screening designed to assess your cardiovascular risk by measuring various types of cholesterol and lipid ratios. A private Cholesterol Test is ideal for anyone concerned about heart health, managing weight, or monitoring the effects of diet and lifestyle changes.

Who Should Get a Private Cholesterol Test? 

A Cholesterol blood test is important for those at risk due to various factors. Consider taking this test if you have:

  • Poor lifestyle habits: High saturated fat diet, smoking, obesity, or heavy alcohol consumption
  • Existing health issues: Diabetes Mellitus, high blood pressure, Thyroid disorder, and liver or kidney disease.
  • Family history of heart disease: Genetic factors can significantly contribute to cholesterol levels, even with a healthy lifestyle.

Non-modifiable risk factors include:

  • Family history of cholesterol-related conditions such as Familial Hypercholesterolaemia (FH)
  • Age: Risk increases as you age
  • Sex: Men have a higher risk of heart disease than women

Why Get Tested for Cholesterol?

Regular blood check for cholesterol level provides important insights about your heart health. Early detection can prevent cardiovascular risks and strokes. If you fall into any of the above categories or want to ensure optimal health, this home cholesterol test is a vital step in managing your well-being.

What Gets Tested

Blood Cholesterol Tests measure your full lipid profile and provides detailed insight into your heart health.

Markers included:

  • LIPP - Lipid Panel, including:
    • CHOL - Total Cholesterol
    • HDL - High-Density Lipoprotein (Good Cholesterol)
    • LDL - Low-Density Lipoprotein (Bad Cholesterol)
    • NHDL - Non-HDL Cholesterol
    • CHR - Total Cholesterol to HDL Ratio
    • TRIG - Triglycerides

THR - Triglyceride to HDL Ratio

column2: PCOS Test: Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome Blood Testing for Women
column3:
Struggling with irregular periods, weight gain, acne, unwanted hair growth, or difficulty getting pregnant? These could be signs of Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome (PCOS).

Our at-home PCOS Test is a simple blood test for PCOS that checks the levels of key hormones in the female body. The Test for Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome can guide you toward better symptom management and fertility support. 

Get reliable results from the comfort of your home with our accurate, hassle-free Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome blood test.

Why Take a PCOS Blood Test?

Polycystic Ovarian Syndrome (PCOS) is a common hormonal condition that can disturb women’s menstrual cycle, impact fertility, and cause symptoms like acne, facial hair, weight gain, fertility issues, and irregular or painful periods. PCOS often runs in families and typically begins showing symptoms after puberty.

Our PCOS blood test checks the key female hormone levels in your body. While this test alone won’t confirm a diagnosis, it offers valuable insight into potential hormonal imbalances, guiding you to take the first step toward symptom relief and long-term management.

What Gets Tested

This panel combines hormone testing with metabolic markers that are commonly disrupted in individuals with PCOS.

  • LH - Luteinizing Hormone
  • TEST - Testosterone (Total)

column2: Coeliac Test | Blood Test for Coeliac Diseases
column3:
The Coeliac Profile is a comprehensive blood test designed to assess gluten intolerance by using various immunological biomarkers, including Total IgA, Tissue Transglutaminase IgA, Gliadin IgA, and Endomysial Antibodies. 

If you have a family history of coeliac disease or are experiencing symptoms like abdominal discomfort, chronic diarrhea, bloating, or fatigue, it is important to undergo a coeliac test. Perfect for those seeking an accurate diagnosis before making the switch to a gluten-free diet. 

Who Should Get Tested?

  • Adults experiencing signs of Coeliac sensitivity.
  • Having a family history of Coeliac disease or being a first-degree relative of a person with Coeliac disease.
  • Individuals suffering from any autoimmune disorder or certain health conditions, such as liver disease, diabetes, selective Immunoglobulin A (IgA) deficiency, or thyroid disorder.
  • Children above the age of 3 years.
  • Children younger than 3 who are at risk of Coeliac disease; however, in this age group, coeliac testing may not give accurate results.

When Should Get Tested?

Coeliac disease is an autoimmune disorder that primarily affects the small intestine, often targeting individuals who are genetically predisposed. If you have a family history of coeliac disease or are experiencing symptoms, it’s important to get a Coeliac screening. Early detection through a coeliac blood test can help prevent complications.

For broader screening, you may also consider our Gluten Sensitivity Test.

What Gets Tested

This test measures four key antibodies associated with gluten sensitivity and coeliac disease.

Markers included:

  • Endomysial Antibodies (IgA)   
  • Tissue Transglutaminase IgA (tTG-IgA)
  • Gliadin IgG Antibodies
  • Total IgA (Immunoglobulin A)

Turnaround Time

  • 7 Days

Beta HCG test (Human Chorionic Gonadotropin blood test) measures the exact level of HCG hormone in the blood, commonly used to confirm and monitor pregnancy. This Quantitative HCG test helps assess pregnancy progression, detect potential complications such as miscarriages and ectopic or molar pregnancies, and identify possible pregnancy-related risks. Additionally, this blood test for beta HCG levels may also be used as a part of a screening test for Down Syndrome during early pregnancy.

Why Consider a Beta HCG Blood Test?

A Beta HCG Test is one of the most reliable ways to detect pregnancy in its earliest stages, often as soon as 10 days after conceiving and earlier than standard urine tests. The high accuracy of the Quantitative blood pregnancy test and its ability to monitor HCG levels over time make it valuable for identifying potential risks and providing informed medical support.

What Gets Tested

Markers included:

  • Beta HCG (Quantitative)

Who Needs a Prostate Blood Test?

  • Men over the age of 50 or

Struggling with infertility, low sex drive, or signs of hormonal imbalance? Our home testosterone test offers a fast, reliable way to check your testosterone levels from the comfort of your home. Using a simple finger prick method, you can get your testosterone hormone level tested with a simple testosterone test kit. Ideal for anyone experiencing symptoms or currently undergoing Testosterone Replacement Therapy. Order online and receive accurate lab results with detailed health insights in just 2–3 days.

Why Get Tested for Testosterone Level?

A testosterone blood test is essential for understanding key aspects of your overall health and reproductive function. In men, the testosterone test helps evaluate processes such as sperm production, muscle strength, libido, and general well-being. Low testosterone levels in men can lead to symptoms like fatigue, depression, reduced sex drive, low libido, and erectile dysfunction.

In women, testosterone hormone testing is important if they are experiencing signs of hormonal imbalance or infertility. Elevated testosterone levels in women can result in the development of male characteristics, such as excessive facial hair growth, deepening of the voice, and irregular menstrual cycles. 

What Gets Tested?

Markers included:

  • Testosterone (Total)

column2: Complete Iron Panel | Blood Test for Iron
column3:
This comprehensive lab test for Iron is designed to assess your body’s iron levels and its ability to store, transport, and utilise iron effectively. It’s ideal for individuals dealing with fatigue, low energy, hair thinning, or those at risk of iron deficiency or overload. With a simple and convenient at-home iron blood test kit, you can easily check your iron levels and identify whether you have too little or too much iron, which can affect your energy levels, immune function, and overall health. 

Why Get Tested?

Iron plays an important role in keeping you healthy and active. Being an essential component of haemoglobin, it plays a key role in the production of Red Blood Cells and supports the transport of oxygen to your organs and tissues. 

Too high or too low an iron level in the body may cause multiple health issues. Low iron levels can lead to symptoms like fatigue, shortness of breath, headaches, and weakness, whereas high levels may cause organ damage over time.

Therefore, getting tested for a complete Iron Panel is necessary to avoid serious health issues and keep yourself healthy and active.

What Gets Tested

Markers included:

  • Iron   
  • Ferritin
  • Transferrin
  • Total Iron Binding Capacity (TIBC)
  • Transferrin Saturation

Turnaround Time

  • 24 Hours

This comprehensive Heart Health Profile helps identify the major risk factors involved in developing cardiovascular problems. The key markers included are complete Cholesterol Profile, CRP, and HbA1c. A heart health exam is ideal for anyone looking to monitor cardiovascular health, especially if you have a family history of heart disease, high cholesterol, or diabetes.

Why Need a Heart Health Profile?

Cardiovascular health issues have become one of the major causes of mortality these days. Sudden heart attacks and strokes are common worldwide, even in the young population. Regular monitoring through heart health tests and scans helps prevent many cardiac issues, thus reducing the risks of severity. 

This advanced Cardiovascular Risk Profile provides detailed insights about the level of inflammation, blood sugar, LDL, HDL, and Triglycerides. Early detection through a comprehensive Heart Blood Test can help reduce cardiovascular issues and other related conditions, such as inflammatory diseases, bacterial infections, and autoimmune disorders.

column2: Allergy Test Profile 1 | 151 Foods & Inhalants IgE
column3:
Struggling with frequent digestive issues, skin irritation, fatigue, or respiratory discomfort? Our Allergy Test Profile 1 is a comprehensive blood allergy test designed to detect IgE-mediated reactions to 151 common foods and inhalants. An allergy profile identifies a wide range of allergens in adults and kids, including dairy, gluten, nuts, fish, as well as pollens and dust mites.

Why Get Tested?

Experiencing congestion, headaches, skin rashes, fatigue, breathing issues, or flu-like symptoms? You may have been exposed to allergens in your environment or diet. The comprehensive Allergy Profile provides insights into IgE-mediated allergies to 151 foods and inhalants.

Unlike food intolerances, IgE allergies trigger immediate and sometimes severe reactions, making early detection critical. By identifying specific allergens through a reliable allergy test, you can make informed lifestyle and dietary adjustments to reduce symptoms and enhance your overall quality of life.

What Gets Tested

The allergy blood tests (Profile-1 and Profile-2) detect IgE (Immunoglobulin E) antibodies, the type involved in immediate allergic reactions, for a wide range of food allergens.

Test includes:

  • 94 common food allergens, such as:
    • Dairy (milk, cheese, casein, whey)
    • Eggs (white & yolk)
    •  Fish & shellfish
    • Nuts and seeds (peanut, walnut, hazelnut, sesame, etc.)
    • Fruits (apple, banana, strawberry, citrus, etc.)
    • Vegetables (carrot, tomato, celery, etc.)
    • Grains (wheat, rye, barley, oats, gluten)
    • Legumes (soy, peas, beans, lentils)
    • Meats & spices

Full list available in the sample report or product brochure

Turnaround Time

  • 4 Days
